findings.fix: Drop the `BaseChange.includeRight_apply` declaration; rely on `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_apply` (or, better, make `BaseChange.includeRight` an `abbrev` so the re-exported name unfolds cleanly).
findings.issue: `includeRight_apply` is `rfl` and unused; the underlying `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_apply` (`(includeRight : B →ₐ[R] A ⊗[R] B) b = 1 ⊗ₜ b`) is the more useful, more general Mathlib simp lemma, and the PR's lemma adds a no-op simp to the set.

findings.fix: Either make it `abbrev HopfAlgebra.baseChange.includeRight := Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := k) (A := K) (B := A)` (so the simp set stays clean), or drop the re-export and have downstream code use `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight` directly.
findings.issue: `BaseChange.includeRight` is a `def` definitionally equal to `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := k) (A := K) (B := A)`; the new name and its `rfl` simp lemma add a layer of indirection on top of an existing Mathlib definition with the same role.

findings.fix: Remove the `@[simp]` attribute (and consider dropping the lemma entirely — `simp [map_comp, BialgHom.coe_comp]` will close the goal in any future consumer).
findings.issue: `map_comp_apply` is unused in the PR and is a strict special case of `BialgHom.coe_comp` after `map_comp`: marking it `@[simp]` adds a redundant simp lemma to the set.

findings.fix: Remove the `@[simp]` attributes on these two lemmas, or drop them entirely; simp will reduce `map` to `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map` and then close via the general lemmas.
findings.issue: `map_toAlgHom` and `map_toCoalgHom` (lines 257) are unused in the PR and are strict special cases of the existing Mathlib `@[simp]` lemmas `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_toAlgHom` and `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_toCoalgHom` (instantiated with `f = BialgHom.id K K`). They add `@[simp]`-tagged duplicates to the simp set at a strictly less general level.

findings.fix: If this general result is needed beyond `BaseChange`, make it public and move it out of `BaseChange.lean` into a Hopf- or bialgebra-hom-specific file (or upstream a version to Mathlib next to `BialgHom`). If it really is BaseChange-local, prove `BaseChange.map_antipode` directly from the tensor-product-Hopf-algebra instance instead of re-deriving the general categorical fact.
findings.issue: `BialgHom.map_antipode` (and the underlying `antipode_comp`) prove the general fact that a bialgebra homomorphism between Hopf algebras preserves the antipode — a Hopf-`BialgHom`-categorical statement that has nothing to do with base change. Hiding it as `private` inside `BaseChange.lean` means the rest of the TauCeti library cannot reuse this general lemma.

findings.fix: If `BaseChange.map_id` / `map_comp` are the only consumers, prove them directly using `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_tmul` (or `map_toAlgHom`/`map_toCoalgHom`) plus extensionality, without first proving the general private lemmas; or, if the general result is wanted, make them public and place them in a bialgebra-tensor-product file.
findings.issue: The `private` lemmas `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_id` and `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_comp` are general monoidal-category facts about `Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map` (the tensor product of identity bialgebra homs is the identity; the tensor product preserves composition) — the right generality is the `BialgCat`/`HopfAlgCat` monoidal structure, not base change. They are placed in `BaseChange.lean` because that is the only consumer; their `private` modifier means the rest of the library cannot reuse them.

summary: The base-change material sits at the right mathematical level, but the file adds four unused `@[simp]` lemmas that duplicate existing general Mathlib simp lemmas in `Bialgebra.TensorProduct` / `Algebra.TensorProduct`, and buries two general categorical facts (bialgebra homs preserve antipode; tensor-product of bialgebra homs preserves identity and composition) as `private` in a base-change file.
